entity.name: Willen LLC
entity.description: Single-founder Delaware LLC operating PHANTOM, an AGPL-3.0-or-later end-to-end encrypted messenger built for restrictive networks. Production-validated 2026-05-07: works on Russian mobile carriers without a VPN, without Orbot, without any third-party app, by way of an embedded Xray VLESS+REALITY transport that masquerades the wire as a TLS handshake to www.microsoft.com.

projects.name: PHANTOM
projects.description: End-to-end encrypted messenger that works in censored networks (Russia confirmed; Iran and China by architectural design) without any VPN or third-party app. Built on the Signal protocol primitives (Double Ratchet, Sealed Sender) with an embedded Xray VLESS+REALITY outer transport for censorship resistance and a Tor v3 onion service as a fallback data plane. Kotlin Multiplatform shared core; Android client today, iOS port in active planning (post-Alpha-2). Self-hostable Rust + Axum relay licensed under AGPL-3.0-or-later — §13 (Remote Network Interaction) makes the relay's privacy promise enforceable against silently-modified hosted forks.

channels.description: GitHub Sponsors — direct support to the maintainer. Please use this channel if your organisation prefers GitHub-native billing.
Fiscal-host backed transparent budgeting. (Registration in progress 2026-05; URL becomes live within Week 1 of the council-revised plan.)
Recurring weekly / monthly donations. (Registration in progress 2026-05.)
Subscription support tier with optional milestone-based bounties. (Registration in progress 2026-05.)
Direct contact for grant programs, large institutional donors, and dual-licensing inquiries.

plans.name: Milestone 1 — iOS port and adaptive transport
plans.description: Twelve months of single-developer work covering: (a) Compose Multiplatform iOS XCFramework build for the existing :shared:core:* modules so PHANTOM ships on iOS at parity with Android; (b) ADR-020 Adaptive Transport Selection — runtime probe-and-pick state machine across direct WSS, Xray REALITY, and Tor (today's choice is build-time only); (c) ADR-021 Multi-server Xray fan-out — closes the single-Hetzner-VPS point-of-failure with two or three operator-controlled servers in different jurisdictions; (d) closure of the four NLnet-blocking security findings (F22 SPK/OPK keystore-wrap, F19+F20 call signalling E2EE, F8 RatchetState wrap, F2+F13 SenderKey signing removal) per the security roadmap.

plans.name: Operational hosting (relay + Xray + future bridges)
plans.description: Recurring infrastructure cost of running the production relay, the Xray REALITY server, the Tor onion service, the WebTunnel bridge, and self-hosted ntfy — all on operator-controlled VPSes. Today this is one Hetzner CPX22 (~€8/month) and one Hetzner CPX21 (~€7/month). Multi-region rollout (ADR-021) raises this proportionally.
